hero_subtitle: SFTY connects fragmented market data and regulatory workflows to help government authorities build structured, evidence-driven supervision and audit-ready oversight.
trust_card_1_value: Market participants, licensing data and risk profiles
trust_card_2_value: Wallets, transactions and exposure across the market
trust_card_3_value: Originator and beneficiary data checked for completeness
trust_card_5_value: On-premise or private cloud for sensitive data
trust_card_6_value: Supervisory outputs, evidence and regulatory reports
problem_description: Government teams struggle with slow, manual compliance processes that rely on fragmented data from multiple sources. This creates gaps in audit trails and weakens the ability to trace supervisory decisions across digital asset markets.
problem_point_1_value: VASP activity, licensing data and risk signals remain scattered
problem_point_2_value: Alerts and investigations depend on disconnected manual processes
problem_point_3_value: Decisions are harder to explain without connected logs and case history
problem_point_4_value: Regulatory teams lack one shared operating layer for supervision
solution_description: SFTY connects fragmented data, provider outputs, case workflows and audit evidence into a unified supervisory layer that enables government teams to manage compliance and oversight with clarity and traceability.
workflow_title: From market signals to supervisory actions
workflow_description: SFTY structures fragmented market signals into prioritized alerts, linked cases, evidence records and supervisory actions inside one transparent government workflow.
workflow_step_1_description: Wallet, VASP, Travel Rule or OSINT signal appears
workflow_step_2_description: SFTY classifies and routes the signal for review
workflow_step_3_description: A supervisory case opens with context & evidence
workflow_step_4_description: Teams record a decision, request or supervisory response
workflow_step_5_description: Evidence, actions and outcomes stay available for review
why_sfty_title: Not another analytics tool. Supervisory control plane
why_sfty_description: SFTY connects fragmented market signals, entity data, cases and evidence into a structured supervisory layer designed for modern government compliance workflows.
why_sfty_card_1_description: See activity across VASPs, wallets, entities and risk signals
why_sfty_card_2_description: Connect participants, structures, links and supervisory records
why_sfty_card_3_description: Move from alerts to investigations, actions and reporting
why_sfty_card_4_description: Understand not only single risks, but the condition of the market
authority_description: SFTY provides government authorities a secure, governed platform with evidence-grade logging, controlled access and procurement-safe deployment for institutional compliance transformation.
authority_card_1_description: Deploy on-premise, in private cloud or inside a controlled government environment
authority_card_2_description: Preserve activity, decisions, case history and reporting evidence
authority_card_3_description: Control who can view, review, escalate and act across teams and agencies
authority_card_4_description: Support phased implementation, documentation, integrations and governance review
ask_sfty_placeholder: How can SFTY support government compliance workflow transformation
ask_sfty_chip_1_message: Explain how supervisory workflows integrate evidence and case data
ask_sfty_chip_2_message: Show how audit logs preserve supervisory decision history securely
ask_sfty_chip_3_message: Describe multi-provider orchestration for institutional compliance oversight
ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for supervisory, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on VASP supervision, sovereign deployment, evidence logging and regulatory workflows.
faq_1_question: What is SFTY for regulators and FIUs?
faq_1_answer: SFTY provides regulators and FIUs with a secure platform that connects market data, VASP profiles, licensing, wallets, transactions, alerts, cases, and reports into one supervisory environment.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY support supervisory workflows?
faq_2_answer: SFTY structures alerts, risk signals and data into cases, links evidence, records decisions and supports a seamless workflow from detection through investigation to supervisory action and reporting.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve supervisory evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves evidence-grade logs, detailed case histories and decision records to support supervisory reviews are audit-ready and can support reporting workflows obligations consistently.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY integrate existing compliance providers?
faq_4_answer: Yes, SFTY operates as a neutral layer above multiple providers, connecting KYC, KYB, KYT, Travel Rule and intelligence outputs without replacing existing compliance tools.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace current analytics or tools?
faq_5_answer: No, SFTY does not replace analytics or compliance tools but aggregates their outputs into a connected supervisory platform with access governance and audit logging.
final_title: Structure government compliance into connected workflows
final_description: Review how SFTY supports multi-provider orchestration, case management and audit-ready supervisory records for institutional oversight.
footer_description: Supervisory and compliance control layer for digital asset markets, businesses and regulated institutions.
footer_contact_title: For supervisory, compliance and deployment discussions.
footer_legal_notice: This page is for informational purposes only and does not constitute legal, regulatory or compliance advice. SFTY deployment depends on jurisdiction, client requirements and applicable laws.
